annotation.definition: Total score on the Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS), a validated 10-item self-report screening tool for perinatal depression.
annotation.collection_method: Self-completed by mother at baseline pregnancy survey during antenatal booking appointment (8-20 weeks gestation).
annotation.units_or_coding: Integer 0–30; scores ≥13 indicate probable major depression.
annotation.study_context: Collected as a key mental health outcome baseline to assess maternal wellbeing and link to Better Start Bradford social-emotional interventions.
annotation.missing_data_notes: May be missing if mother declined mental health screening or questionnaire incomplete.
annotation.source_citation: Dickerson et al. 2016 (PMC4996273) — Methods: Data collection, Maternal mental health

paper_source: Dickerson et al. 2016, BMC Public Health, PMC4996273

annotation.definition: Body Mass Index calculated from self-reported pre-pregnancy weight and measured height, expressed as kg/m².
annotation.collection_method: Derived from self-reported pre-pregnancy weight (kg) and height measured at recruitment; collected at antenatal booking.
annotation.units_or_coding: Continuous float, kg/m²; WHO categories: <18.5 underweight, 18.5–24.9 normal, 25–29.9 overweight, ≥30 obese.
annotation.study_context: Core variable for the nutrition and obesity domain; used to assess baseline maternal adiposity and stratify intervention impact.
annotation.missing_data_notes: Missing if height not measurable or weight self-report refused; pre-pregnancy weight is self-reported and subject to recall bias.
annotation.source_citation: Dickerson et al. 2016 (PMC4996273) — Methods: Baseline data collection, Physical health measures

paper_source: Dickerson et al. 2016, BMC Public Health, PMC4996273
